flat_text: Hello. Hello. Hello there, it's Dr. Steven here. How can I help you this afternoon? Uh, I've just got this terrible headache since midday, um, on the left side. Uh, well, you know, I noticed some zigzag lines in my vision a few minutes before the headache started. Mm-hmm. My vision blurred. Um, you know, it's just— I, yeah, pain's just horrible. It's just making me feel so unwell. Um. Mm-hmm. Okay. I'm afraid of my work, and I just want you to do something. Well, let's try our best. Let's try and get you— let's try and get you well soon. Um, you mentioned it's on your left-hand side, is that right? And it started at midday a few hours ago? Yeah. Okay. Um, did the pain come on quite suddenly, or was it more of a gradual build-up? Uh, no, really. It just happened. Okay. And are you able to describe what kind of headache it was? For example, was it throbbing, or was it more of a sharp pain? Yeah, I guess it's— yeah, I guess it's throbbing, um, on that left side. And is it moving anywhere else at all? No, but it's worse when I move. Okay. Is that when you move your neck? Well, like, if I lean forward or, like, move my head or neck, yeah, the headache gets worse. Okay. Um. I'm just really worried. Like, I've just been googling and, like, I've read that, you know, if your headache gets worse when you move, it could be a sign of brain cancer. Okay. Is that what you're worried about? Sure. Is that— is that what your main worry is today? Oh, yeah. Yeah. And I've, like, you know, my— my friend's mum, she— she recently died of a— of a brain tumor, so it's really concerning me, actually. Oh dear. I'm sorry to hear that. I can— I can understand why it's affecting you. Um, but why don't we— why don't we take a good history first, and let me examine you, and then we can have a little chat afterwards and see what's going on here. Okay. Um, so you mentioned a few things. You mentioned the headache, and you also mentioned some zigzag lines, uh, before the headaches came on. And your vision has been blurry as well. Is that right? Is that on the left. Yeah. Is that on both eyes, or just the left— left eye? It feels like it's on both. Both eyes. Okay. Have you had any difficulty with seeing bright lights at all? Yeah, well, I'm wearing— I'm wearing sunglasses right now. It's just, you know, light's really hurting my eyes. Oh, I see. Okay. Um, and you mentioned that you felt quite nauseous. Have you vomited at all? Yeah. I vomited twice. Twice. Okay. And this earlier today. Okay. Um, any temperatures or fevers? Um, no, I don't feel feverish. Okay. Um, any other funny skin rashes that you may have noticed? No, I haven't noticed anything like that. Okay. Um, fine. Um, and do you normally get headaches? Um, not really prone to them, no. No, but I did— I did have a similar headache a few weeks ago. Okay. And what happened then? Just similar. How long did it last for? A couple of days. Did you do anything that made it better? Um, yeah, I had to take painkillers and had to sort of go to bed and rest and. Yeah. Had a couple days off work, and I work in the fashion industry, and, you know, they're wondering what's going on, like, having to take sick days. Yeah. And I need to get back to work today, but, you know, what if it is a brain tumor? What's going to happen with my future, my career? You know. Yeah. Yeah. I'm really worried about this. I've never had headaches like this before. Like, why should I get them now? Yeah, sure. I mean, I can see your concerns. Obviously, there's a lot— there's a lot of worry there. Um, but, you know, let— let— let's take it step by step, um, and see exactly what the cause of these headaches are first. Um, I'm just going to ask you some, um, very, uh, yes/no questions, if that's okay. Um, um, um, have you noticed any problems with your speech at all? Any difficulty with your words? No. Any problems with your arms and legs, for example, numbness or weakness? No. And any difficulty with balance— your balance or coordination? No. Okay. And have you had any injuries to your head? Have you had a fall recently or been knocked on the head? No. Okay. All right. Um, and— and you're feeling otherwise okay? So, for example, your, um, uh, your— your breathing's okay, you're not feeling breathless, or? No. No. Okay. Um, do you have any other medical history I should be aware of? No. No? Nothing. So, for example, have you had— has anyone told you you've had migraines in the past? No. No. Okay. Do you? No, because my mum— my mum has— has migraines, but I've— I've not been diagnosed. Right. So my next question was about family history. Is there anything in the family? So, apart from migraines, is there anything else? Anything related to the brain, for example? Thyroid as well. What was that, sorry? My mum has an underactive thyroid as well. Oh, underactive thyroid as well. Okay. Um, okay. Any other problems with, for example, the brain or, um, you mentioned brain cancer. Any other family history of that? No. No. Okay. Um, in terms of medications, do you take anything on a regular basis? Um, I take the pill, microgynon. Okay. And how long have you been on that for? Uh, three months. Three months. Okay. And is that mainly for contraception, or is that for something else? No, it's for contraception. Okay. All right. That's something we may need to have a little discussion about today as well. Um, uh, well, my next question was about, um, your social— really, your situation at home. You mentioned you work in the fashion industry. Is your work— is your job quite stressful at the moment? Yeah, it's really stressful, actually. Have you? Just having problems with, like, people at work. You know, managers are like putting a lot of pressure on me to deliver and. Uh, yeah. It's not a good time. Is it getting you down? I don't feel down. I just feel a bit stressed. Okay. All right. And what kind of things do you do to alleviate your stress normally? Nothing, really. Okay. Do you do much in the way of exercise? No. No. Okay. Um, who's at home with you? Um, I live on my own. Okay. Um, are you coping okay at the moment? Fine. Yeah? Okay. Uh, do you smoke at all? No. At all? And do you drink much in the way of alcohol? No. Okay. All right. Um, so normally, at least, I'd like to kind of proceed to examination. Um, but, um, based on your story, um, I wonder whether you may, um, may have a migraine, uh, uh, migraine-type headache. Uh, it sounds, um, very classical of that of that nature, the, the, the, the headache on one side, throbbing nature, the nausea, vomiting, um, sensitivity to bright lights. Uh, I'm reassured that you don't have anything more serious, such as meningitis or, indeed, brain cancer. I know that was one of the big worries of yours initially. Oh, yeah. Thank you, Alex. Uh, thanks so much. I was so worried that I had cancer and, oh, now you say migraine. Yeah, I guess my mum had similar symptoms to me, and. Yeah. Yeah, it does make sense. Well, I hope that puts your, you know, ear of mine at ease. Um, certainly, this is the second migraine you've had in the space of a month. Um, so I think moving forward, there's a couple of options. I think keeping a headache diary would be quite important, uh, to see exactly what's triggering this headache. But we should also try and treat your headaches today. So, some strong painkillers would be advisable. So, you know, think about paracetamol or even co-codamol, um, and some anti-inflammatories such as ibuprofen and naproxen. How does that sound to you? Okay, that sounds good. Um, and, uh, you know, if your headaches were to become more persistent, then we can maybe think about giving you some medications that will stop your headaches from happening in the first place, something, you know, prophylactic medicines. Okay. And then we can have a discussion next time. I'll see you next week, perhaps. Okay. Okay? Um, now, the couple of things I want you to think about is, you know, if your symptoms aren't getting any better in the next two, three days, or indeed are getting worse, I'd like you to come back and see me, um, straight away if possible. So if your headache's getting worse and not responding to medications, or your, um, your neck pain is getting worse, or you're developing any weakness in your arms and legs, or your vision, then I want you to come back and see me straight away. Okay? Okay. Does that sound all okay? Doctor, that's great. Great. Any questions for me? No. Thank you. Okay. Have a good day. Okay, thanks. Bye. Bye.
